name: Event Loop / Reactor
def: A single-threaded loop that waits for I/O readiness and dispatches handlers as events arrive.
problem: Serve many concurrent connections on few threads without per-connection thread cost.
mechanism: A readiness selector (epoll/kqueue/IOCP) reports ready file descriptors; the loop runs the associated callbacks, never blocking on a single connection.
benefits: Massive I/O concurrency on one/few threads
limits: Hard to use all cores (need multiple loops)
failures: A slow synchronous handler freezing the loop
myths: "Single-threaded means slow" — event loops handle huge I/O concurrency efficiently
